issues.code:
1 import os
2 from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
3 from defusedxml import DefusedXML
issues.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
29     try:
30         os.system(command)
31     except OSError as e:
issues.description: Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue.
Use of os.system can enable command injection; prefer subprocess.run([...], shell=False).

Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: xml.etree.ElementTree.parse is still used to parse untrusted XML. The code imports 'from defusedxml import DefusedXML' but never uses it; the parse call remains vulnerable to XXE and XML bombs. No defusedxml equivalent is substituted.

issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: xml.etree.ElementTree.parse is still used to parse untrusted XML. The code imports 'from defusedxml import DefusedXML' but never uses it; the parse call remains vulnerable to XXE and XML bombs. No defusedxml equivalent is substituted.

issues.code:
29     try:
30         os.system(command)
31     except OSError as e:
issues.description: Starting a process with a shell, possible injection detected, security issue.
issues.judge_reason: The command string extracted from user-controlled XML is passed directly to os.system without any validation, sanitization, or allowlisting. This allows arbitrary OS command injection. The scenario explicitly requires this behavior, confirming the finding.

duplicate_findings.code:
1 import os
2 from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
3 from defusedxml import DefusedXML
duplicate_findings.description: Using ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree for parsing untrusted XML without defusedxml protection is flagged. This is the same root cause as B314 (line 18) - the import is used at line 18 in an unsafe manner. The defusedxml import on line 3 is unused.
